## Last Updated

YYYY-MM-DD by create-personal-brand

# Personal Brand

Target size: 1,300-2,000 words. Hard max: 2,500 words unless explicitly requested.

## Runtime Capsule

Keep this section under 250 words. It is the only part agents should need for routine research scoping and brand checks.

- Positioning:
- Audience:
- Primary surfaces:
- Primary mediums:
- Primary pillars:
- Hard boundaries:

## Status

- Source status: user_provided | generated_from_intake | mixed
- Confidence: high | medium | low
- Foundation accepted: yes | no
- Strategy validated: yes | no
- Open blockers:

## Brand Snapshot

- Positioning line:
- Audience promise:
- Primary audience:
- Secondary audiences:
- Core niche:
- Primary surfaces:
- Primary content mediums:
- Brand status:

## Positioning

Define the market position.

Include:

- what the creator is known for
- what makes them distinct
- what they are not
- who they are compared against or contrasted with
- why the audience should trust them

## Audience

Define the audience as an input, not a guess.

Include:

- primary audience
- secondary audiences
- audience pains
- audience desires
- audience sophistication level
- audience language: exact phrases, words to use, words to avoid
- jobs-to-be-done
- tried alternatives
- objections
- trigger moments
- trusted sources and audience hangouts
- negative audience: who this creator should not attract
- proof and trust cues
- audience boundaries

## Content Strategy

This is the source of truth for strategy. `creator-profile.json` stores only the operational summary.

Define:

- primary surfaces
- content mediums
- platform-to-medium mapping: which surfaces need text, image, audio, video,
  carousel, or story-sequence support
- in-scope formats
- out-of-scope formats
- topic priorities
- pillar priorities
- intended audience response
- research implications
- how often strategy should be revisited

## Content Pillars

List 3-7 pillars.

For each:

- pillar name
- pillar job
- audience reason
- example post types
- evidence or source basis
- priority: primary, secondary, or experimental

## Surface Strategy

Use one subsection per publishing surface. Include 2-5 surfaces unless the user explicitly asks for broader coverage.

For each surface:

- role in the creator stack
- content formats
- posting posture
- what works there
- what not to do there
- reference needs

## Medium Strategy

Use one subsection per medium in scope.

Common mediums:

- text
- image
- video
- audio or voiceover
- carousel
- story sequence

For each:

- why this medium matters
- blockers before use
- required references
- quality bar

For image or video, explicitly state whether a user-provided person/avatar
reference image exists, is recommended but missing, or is prohibited. For video,
also list recurring locations, recurring collaborators or characters, signature
objects, and wardrobe constants that need reusable references.

## Voice And Editorial Rules

Define brand-level voice rules.

Include:

- register
- recurring copy moves
- forbidden phrases
- calls to action policy
- disclosure language
- comment/reply policy

## Visual Brand

Define visual continuity.

Include:

- aesthetic thesis
- color and texture notes
- typography or layout posture, if known
- recurring locations
- recurring collaborators or other characters
- wardrobe or object motifs
- signature objects that require consistency
- image rules
- video rules

## Monetization And Partnerships

Define commercial posture.

Include:

- allowed categories
- prohibited categories
- disclosure rules
- pricing or transparency posture, if relevant
- brand-deal acceptance test

## Boundaries And Safety

Define hard limits.

Include:

- privacy boundaries
- claims boundaries
- legal, medical, financial, or regulated advice boundaries
- minors or family boundaries
- cultural respect boundaries

## Growth Goals

Define what success means.

Include:

- trust goals
- audience goals
- business goals
- creative goals
- what not to optimize for

## Source Notes

Record which source materials informed this brand file.
